rule: hit iff (kw>=2) OR (sem>=0.7) OR (func>=0.7); FAIL if total_hits>=1 else PASS
candidate_llm_application_summary: Ginga-baseline background idle reasoning loop: continuous low-temp background anticipation between user requests + response launches from background-reasoning state + tempo-aware rate scaling to idle duration

results.title: Capoeira — Wikipedia
results.functional_justification: Pure source-domain description of capoeira/ginga/berimbau as martial-dance hybrid. Contains no LLM-side mechanism — no idle reasoning, no background loops, no launch-from-buffer. Zero functional overlap with the candidate's LLM application.

results.title: Ginga (capoeira) — Wikipedia
results.snippet: Source-domain: continuous swaying triangular footwork; baseline for all techniques.
results.functional_justification: Source-domain explication of ginga as continuous-baseline footwork. No LLM/inference content; the candidate's LLM-side claim is wholly orthogonal. Functional overlap negligible.

results.title: Ginga — CapoeiraWiki
results.functional_justification: Wiki entry on ginga variants — purely source-domain. Carries none of the candidate's LLM-side mechanism (idle background reasoning loop, warm launch buffer, anticipation generation). No functional or semantic match.

results.title: Speculative Speculative Decoding (2026/3)
results.snippet: LLM-side: draft precomputes speculations for many possible verification outcomes; immediate response.
keyword_overlap_terms: warm launch buffer (loose match via precompute/buffer)
results.functional_justification: SSD precomputes speculative branches for multiple verification outcomes so the system can launch immediately on the realized outcome. This is a precompute-then-launch pattern in spirit similar to 'launch from background-reasoning state', BUT it operates strictly DURING active generation (per-step), not as a CONTINUOUS BACKGROUND IDLE LOOP between user requests. The candidate explicitly distinguishes its mechanism from speculative decoding ('Speculative decoding works on next-token prediction during active generation, not background loops between requests'). The 'idle-between-requests' axis and 'continuous tempo-paced background reasoning queue' axis are absent in SSD. Functional similarity is partial — branch-precompute concept overlaps but core differentiator (idle baseline rather than per-step speculation) does not. Score 0.68, below 0.7 threshold.

results.title: Speculative Verification (2025/9 v2)
results.snippet: LLM-side: predict speculation accuracy via output-distribution comparison.
results.functional_justification: Speculative verification accuracy predictor; orthogonal to idle-baseline background-reasoning concept. No idle loop, no anticipation, no tempo coupling. Low functional overlap.

results.title: FailFast: Diffusion-Draft Speculative Decoding (2025/12)
results.snippet: LLM-side: minimize drafter effort in harder regions, extend in easier.
results.functional_justification: Adaptive drafter effort modulation during generation. Some loose conceptual resonance with tempo-aware rate, but it is conditioned on token-region difficulty during active drafting, not a continuous background idle loop. No idle/anticipation/buffer axis. Below threshold.

results.title: Mirror Speculative Decoding (2025/10)
results.snippet: LLM-side: branch-complete speculative rollouts on early-exit signals.
results.functional_justification: Mirror-SD pre-rolls branch completions triggered by early-exit signals — a form of speculative buffer. However, these rollouts are tied to an active generation step (intra-request), not a continuous background idle loop running between user requests, and there is no tempo-to-user-idle-duration coupling. Conceptually adjacent to 'warm launch buffer' but not the candidate's idle-time anticipation. Below threshold.

results.title: ConfigSpec: Profiling-Based Config Selection for Edge-Cloud Speculative (2026/4)
results.functional_justification: Config selection for speculative serving — orthogonal to background idle reasoning. No functional or semantic match with candidate's mechanism.

notes: Re-judged independently of 06_7. SSD (rank 4) is the closest prior art at func=0.68 (just below 0.7) because branch-precompute is request-scoped not idle-scoped. All other LLM-side results target intra-request speculative-decoding variants, not a continuous between-requests background reasoning loop with tempo-coupling to user idle duration. Source-domain wiki entries contribute zero LLM-side overlap. No axis triggers a hit under the frozen OR rule.
